tax: Smoulder Cinderling is placed at species rank beneath Cinderling (Pyric order, Fire Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a low-oxygen smouldering body, near-invisibility until fed air, and an ash-creep trail. It is separated from its sibling the Ember Flameling by its hidden smokeless smoulder versus the Flameling's bright leaping flame; the two share the genus body-plan but never the open visible flame. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: slow smokeless combustion creeping through detritus at low oxygen.
ap: Smoulder Cinderling stands indefinite, a creeping front up to several metres wide. Its silhouette is a glowing edge advancing through mat, with no fixed shape. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a dull red line of heat with little or no flame; a creeping band of grey ash left behind the glowing front; and a faint warmth and acrid smell in still air before any glow is seen. It moves with a slow creep along combustible mat and dust, quickening into open flame if a draught reaches it, and shows a faint dull-red line, easily missed. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a band of grey ash through fungal mat and a slow-spreading warm zone, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Smoulder Cinderling persists in deep fungal mats, dust-floors, and peat-like detritus in low-oxygen galleries where slow combustion can hide. It does not breed; it forms where fire-pressure ignites a slow smoulder in low-oxygen detritus, and recurs whenever fresh mat regrows over the burn and the smoulder re-seeds. It sustains its mass on slow combustion of detritus at low oxygen, drawing in fungal mat, dust, and peat-like debris. Range is bounded by the extent of combustible mat: outside that band the body dies on bare stone or in standing water. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a patch of mat that warms and greys without visible flame, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Smoulder Cinderling is roused by a draught of fresh air, which flares it to open flame and otherwise lies a near-invisible warm glow in the mat. It claims a single mat-floored gallery and answers intrusion with flaring into open flame when air reaches it, igniting all around. Toward grey-folk it is a hidden trap underfoot; survey crews report that Beast-Bonded test mat with a damp rod, since a Cinderling burns boots before it is seen. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Smoulder Cinderling: a body of slow smokeless combustion creeping through detritus at low oxygen; immune to fire and smothering at depth, where little oxygen reaches it; and a defining weakness — flooding and the removal of its mat fuel. Its senses run through the medium itself: heat and air-gradient sensing through the mat. It tolerates darkness and low oxygen, which it prefers but fails at flooding and fuel-removal. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the creeping glow-front and ash-band, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Smoulder Cinderling are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it spreads unseen through mat and dust; it flares to open flame when fed a draught; it reignites from buried embers after seeming dead; and it heats the floor enough to burn through boots. Each is a direct expression of slow low-oxygen combustion of detritus, so it cannot, for instance, leap, light a passage, cross bare stone, or survive flooding. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Smoulder Cinderling matters because it burns crews from below before it is seen, and mat is now tested with a damp rod before camp is made. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a hidden underfoot fire hazard logged with mat galleries, and its presence reshapes routes through the fungal-mat galleries, now rod-tested and never camped on warm mat. A grey ash-band creeping through living mat marks an active Cinderling front.
g: Smoulder Cinderling (Ignis latens) is a creeping fire elemental of smokeless smoulder that hides in fungal mat and dust and flares only when fed air. Documented from the deep fungal mats and dust-floors of low-oxygen galleries, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a dull red glow buried in slow-burning detritus, almost smokeless — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. It is the patient, hidden sibling of the Flameling, spreading unseen through mat until a draught wakes it to open flame. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a creeping band of grey ash through fungal mat with no open flame seen and no carcass.
